title: stephanotis isaura
intro: Stephanotis isaura, commonly known as the Star of Bethlehem or Orphan Jessamine, is a flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits such as reducing anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation, and aiding in sleep. The therapeutic actions of Stephanotis isaura include sedative and anxiolytic effects, which are attributed to its bioactive constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides. Research has identified the presence of bioactive compounds like scopoletin, which is responsible for the plant's sedative and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects: Stephanotis isaura can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, due to its high concentration of cardiac glycosides. Ingestion of the plant can also lead to cardiac arrhythmias and palpitations as a result of its toxic compounds affecting the heart. Furthermore, improper use of Stephanotis isaura may cause dizziness and headaches, as well as allergic reactions in some individuals. If not handled correctly, the plant's sap may also cause skin irritation and other adverse reactions.
